evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` exposes deprecated facade symbols while nearby modules in this batch call different reporter layers directly (`presentationReporter` in `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` vs `coreReporter` in `useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`).

Multiple MediaGalleryItem-layer modules (`InfoTooltip.tsx`, `ShotActions.tsx`, `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`) import `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` from `../../MediaGallery/types`, so lower-level item modules depend on a sibling aggregate module instead of a neutral domain/shared type boundary.

`src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx` depends on singleton exports (`toastManager`, `toast`) from the toast runtime surface, reinforcing implicit global UI runtime coupling rather than explicit local ownership.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts defines both UseSegmentSlotModeInput and UseSegmentSlotModeResolvedProps, then resolveUseSegmentSlotModeInput() performs mostly 1:1 field forwarding across nested bags (scope/data/structureVideo/actions) before hook logic runs.
src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts exports useUpdatingTimestamp() as a pure pass-through to useRelativeTimestamp() with identical options surface and no behavior change.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx constructs multiple large memoized model objects that primarily repackage existing nested state into another shape, increasing indirection at call boundaries.

Task contract keys are duplicated across client payload builders (src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts) and server completion/cost readers (supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts, supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts, supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts), creating multi-module schema lockstep.

src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts (data freshness/polling core) imports UI toast runtime from src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx, coupling non-UI coordination logic to presentation-layer dependency.

src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts depends on realtime freshness side-effect helpers plus query/polling controls; this reinforces broad coupling around task fetching behavior rather than a narrow dependency boundary.

The public `generateVideo()` orchestration path in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts` (DB read, payload assembly, task creation, error mapping) is not exercised in `generateVideoService.test.ts`, which focuses on underscored helper exports.

`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts` now returns `Promise<OperationResult<SettingsFetchResult>>`, but `src/shared/lib/__tests__/toolSettingsService.test.ts` still asserts the pre-OperationResult contract (`result.settings` and thrown errors), leaving current caller behavior weakly validated.

`scheduleAdjacentGenerationPagePreload` (debounce timer + cancel function) in `src/features/gallery/lib/adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts` is not covered by `adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.test.ts`, which only tests cleanup and direct prefetch.

Failure contracts vary across neighboring shared APIs: status-union return objects in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`, `OperationResult` envelopes in `src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts` and `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts`, and throw-based APIs in `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/presentationReporter.ts` (`presentAndRethrow`).

`parseJsonBodyStrict` accepts `ParseJsonBodyOptions` including `mode`, but internally hardcodes strict mode and ignores caller-supplied `mode`, creating a misleading surface (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts`).

The mismatch is already reflected in consumer tests (`src/shared/lib/__tests__/toolSettingsService.test.ts`) that still consume old throw/value semantics, suggesting API boundaries are hard to predict.

Multiple assigned files use large explanatory blocks that restate straightforward code behavior (for example `src/shared/lib/timelinePositionCalculator.ts`, `src/shared/lib/typeGuards.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/taskTypeUtils.ts`) rather than documenting non-obvious constraints.

`src/shared/lib/galleryUtils.ts` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` include long narrative comments around simple data-shape declarations, producing high comment-to-logic noise characteristic of generated-style ceremony.

`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` and `src/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems.ts` are deprecated compatibility re-exports, while repository search shows no active imports from these legacy shared-hook paths.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` marks `handleError`/`handleAndRethrow` as deprecated with removal target `2026-06-30`, but active call sites across the codebase still invoke `handleError(...)` broadly.

`src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` is a deprecated shim, and imports still exist (`src/shared/lib/__tests__/editVideoDefaults.test.ts`, `src/shared/lib/__tests__/joinClipsDefaults.test.ts`, `src/tools/edit-video/settings/__tests__/editVideoDefaults.test.ts`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/settings.test.ts`).

Travel-between-images still exposes legacy migration boundary types (`LegacyStructureVideoConfig`, `TravelBetweenImagesLegacyTaskInput`) in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` and depends on `legacyStructureVideo.ts` compatibility contracts.

`src/shared/hooks/` is a flat, high-mix directory (`file_count: 77`) while assigned files include both domain-migration shims (`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx`, `useDerivedItems.ts`) and active feature hooks (`useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts`), indicating mixed ownership in one package.

`useEditToolMediaPersistence` has two full implementations in different locations (`src/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts`) plus wrapper exports under `src/tools/edit-media/hooks/`, creating ambiguous canonical placement.

`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` is heavily coupled to the flat hooks folder (`coupling_matrix: src/shared/hooks/__tests__/ → src/shared/hooks/ = 75`), and includes `useEditVideoSettings.test.ts` even though the production hook lives under `src/shared/settings/hooks/`.
`useTimelineOrchestrator` combines viewport math, drag/drop, selection, tap-to-move, structure-video placement, and UI modal state in one hook with a very wide input and return surface.
`useVideoEditing` mixes timeline selection geometry, validation, LoRA/resource loading, persisted settings integration, and task-triggering behavior in a single hook.
`useProjectCRUD` combines initial bootstrap/onboarding setup, CRUD data access, UI toast/error handling, and user-preference side effects inside one controller hook.
`checkOrchestratorCompletion` in the edge function coordinates state-machine gating, sibling lookup policy, final-step checks, status transitions, and billing persistence in one module.
supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts enforces authenticateRequest and explicit service-role gating before cost logic.
supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts and supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts both assert auth context with assertCompletionAuthContext at function entry.
authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Client-side hooks in reviewed scope (for example useTasks/useGenerationMutations patterns) still rely primarily on downstream data-layer enforcement; explicit auth-intent checks are not uniformly visible at call boundaries.

evidence: UI primitive/theme siblings drift on quote/style conventions: src/shared/components/ui/theme/button.tsx and src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx use double-quote style while src/shared/components/ui/primitives/cn.ts and src/shared/components/ui/primitives/tooltip.tsx use single-quote style.
The inconsistency appears inside the same UI subsystem, creating a style island rather than a boundary-based convention.
convention_outlier.issues_preventing_higher_score: Formatting convention drift in tightly related files increases review noise and weakens predictability for future edits.

evidence: src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts marks handleError/handleAndRethrow as deprecated, but active callers still use them (e.g., src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts, src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts, src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts).

Nearby sibling logic uses a different error pipeline directly (src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts imports reportError/reportAndRethrow from coreReporter), creating parallel reporting contracts.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Two active error-reporting surfaces with different call patterns remain in production paths, increasing behavioral divergence risk (toast behavior, normalization, and rethrow semantics).

evidence: src/features/gallery/hooks/useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts sets totalPages to Infinity when totalItems is unknown.

src/features/gallery/lib/adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts computes hasNextPage as currentPage < totalPages, which is always true with Infinity; this keeps forward prefetch logic active even when actual data may have ended.
logic_clarity.issues_preventing_higher_score: Pagination termination for unknown totals is implicit and potentially unbounded, making control flow harder to reason about under empty/failing next-page responses.

evidence: src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts uses non-null assertion effectiveProjectId! inside queryFn despite nullable type at function boundary.
src/shared/hooks/timeline/timelineFrameCalculators.ts relies on non-null assertions (timeline_frame!) and index assumptions (draggedShotGens[0]) that are not encoded as non-empty/narrowed types.
type_safety.issues_preventing_higher_score: Type contracts are partially bypassed with non-null assertions instead of expressing invariants in function signatures and guards, leaving runtime failure modes under-specified.

findings.summary: Error handling is split across parallel facades, creating boundary drift and hub coupling.
evidence: `handleError.ts` still exports deprecated compatibility functions that proxy to `presentationReporter`.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` imports `reportError/reportAndRethrow` from `coreReporter`, while `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` imports `presentError` from `presentationReporter` directly.
Callers in the same feature area are coupled to different internal error layers, so changing one layer requires broad coordination.
findings.suggestion: Define one public runtime error API (single entrypoint module) and migrate feature callers to it; keep internal reporters private, then remove deprecated facade exports on the planned removal path.

findings.summary: MediaGalleryItem modules depend on sibling MediaGallery aggregate types, creating reverse coupling.
evidence: All three files import `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` from `../../MediaGallery/types`.
Item-level modules therefore depend on a sibling aggregate module (`MediaGallery`) for core data contracts.
This creates a fragile boundary: refactors in MediaGallery type organization can cascade into MediaGalleryItem internals.
findings.suggestion: Move `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` (and related core gallery item contracts) into a neutral shared/domain type module, then invert imports so both MediaGallery and MediaGalleryItem consume that shared contract.

findings.summary: Nested input/resolved interfaces add ceremony without reducing complexity.
evidence: useSegmentSlotMode() immediately calls resolveUseSegmentSlotModeInput(), which copies many fields from grouped bags into another flat object with mostly identical names.
Caller-side state in ShotImageManagerContainer and Lightbox flows is already structured; additional remapping layer does not enforce new invariants or validation.
This extra abstraction creates two public shapes to maintain and expands change surface when adding/removing one field.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one public input contract: either keep grouped bags and consume them directly inside useSegmentSlotMode, or keep a single flat interface and remove resolveUseSegmentSlotModeInput(). Add only targeted derived helpers for real transformations (for example, location-state parsing), not full-structure forwarding.

findings.summary: Thin wrapper exports create API surface without behavioral leverage.
evidence: useUpdatingTimestamp() is a direct pass-through to useRelativeTimestamp(options) with no compatibility translation, policy, or defaults differing from the underlying hook.
Auth hooks mostly delegate to runAuthSupabaseOperation while adding little distinct domain behavior, resulting in several near-wrapper hooks with similar callback scaffolding.
These wrappers increase discovery cost and make behavior tracing harder because the call stack gains extra layers that do not transform contracts.
findings.suggestion: Prune zero-leverage wrappers and expose one canonical hook/function per behavior family. Keep wrapper exports only where they encode stable alias semantics or default policy differences; otherwise inline call sites to the canonical function.

findings.summary: Task schema is duplicated across client builders and edge readers.
evidence: Client task creators emit many raw key contracts (e.g., orchestrator_details, run_id, segment/join counters, structure and lineage fields) as ad-hoc object literals.
Edge completion/cost code re-parses those keys with fallback heuristics and per-task branching, indicating no shared typed contract package.
Any field rename or shape drift requires coordinated edits across separate runtimes (browser TS and Deno edge TS), increasing regression risk and dependency churn.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared task-contract module (schema + encoder/decoder) imported by both client task builders and edge handlers. Centralize canonical keys and fallbacks there, then make each task-specific module depend on that contract instead of open-coded object literals.

findings.summary: Core freshness manager depends directly on UI toast runtime dependency.
evidence: DataFreshnessManager imports toast from UI runtime and emits user-facing notifications inside core polling/failure logic.
useTasks and other data hooks rely on freshness manager behavior, so UI dependency leaks into data coordination path.
This ties non-visual infrastructure to presentation dependencies and complicates isolated testing/reuse of freshness logic.
findings.suggestion: Invert the dependency: keep DataFreshnessManager UI-agnostic and publish typed failure events/callbacks; attach toast handling in a UI adapter subscribed at app boundary.

findings.summary: Tool settings tests still target old throw/value API instead of current OperationResult contract
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` returns `Promise<OperationResult<SettingsFetchResult>>` (`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts:324-329`) and returns failures via `toOperationFailure(...)` in catch paths (`:373-420`).

Tests assert direct `result.settings` (`toolSettingsService.test.ts:131-138`) and `rejects.toThrow(...)` for auth/abort (`:157-163`, `:175-180`), which does not validate the current `ok/value/errorCode` envelope contract.
findings.suggestion: Rewrite `toolSettingsService.test.ts` to assert `result.ok`, `result.value`, and failure `errorCode/policy/recoverable` branches, and add regression cases for cancellation/auth/network mapping using the OperationResult shape.

findings.summary: Large `generateVideo()` orchestration path is untested while tests cover only helper internals
evidence: The public API `generateVideo(params)` includes validation, mutation settling, DB fetch, payload construction, and task submission (`generateVideoService.ts:562+`).
Test file imports helper/test-only exports (`_stripModeFromPhaseConfig`, `_buildTravelRequestBodyV2`, etc.) and contains no `generateVideo(` test invocation.

The test mock for `@/integrations/supabase/client` provides `supabase` rather than `getSupabaseClient`, so the real runtime dependency path used by `generateVideoService.ts` (`getSupabaseClient as supabase`) is not validated.
findings.suggestion: Add focused integration-style unit tests for `generateVideo()` with a `getSupabaseClient` mock that exercises success, fetch failure, insufficient images, and task-creation failure branches, while keeping helper tests as secondary coverage.

findings.summary: Debounce/cancel behavior for adjacent-page preload scheduler is not validated
evidence: `scheduleAdjacentGenerationPagePreload` wraps prefetch in a debounced `setTimeout` and returns a cancellation function (`adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.ts:90-125`).

Current tests cover `cleanupDistantGenerationPages` and `prefetchAdjacentGenerationPages` (`adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.test.ts`), but do not assert debounce timing or cancellation semantics of the scheduler.
findings.suggestion: Add fake-timer tests that verify (1) prefetch runs after debounce delay, (2) returned cleanup cancels pending execution, and (3) multiple rapid schedules coalesce to expected call count.

findings.summary: Related APIs use incompatible failure channels (status objects, OperationResult, and throw)
evidence: `generationTaskRepository` encodes failures in `status` fields (e.g., `query_failed`, `scope_mismatch`) while still returning fulfilled promises (`generationTaskRepository.ts:57-168`, `:186-249`).
`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` uses `OperationResult` envelopes (`toolSettingsService.ts:324-420`).
`presentAndRethrow` throws (`presentationReporter.ts:26-27`) and `edgeRequest` also uses `OperationResult` plus response helpers (`edgeRequest.ts:138-219`).
findings.suggestion: Define one boundary rule per layer (for example: internal helpers throw typed errors, boundary adapters return OperationResult) and add thin adapters where needed so sibling APIs in the same subsystem expose a predictable failure model.

findings.summary: `parseJsonBodyStrict` exposes an option field it intentionally ignores
evidence: `ParseJsonBodyOptions` includes `mode` (`edgeRequest.ts:132-136`).
`parseJsonBodyStrict(..., options)` hardcodes `mode: 'strict'` and only forwards message fields (`edgeRequest.ts:193-202`), so `options.mode` is silently ignored.
Tests pass `{ mode: 'strict' }` into `parseJsonBodyStrict` (`edgeRequest.test.ts:43`, `:57`), reinforcing a misleading API signature.
findings.suggestion: Change `parseJsonBodyStrict` options type to omit `mode` (e.g., `Omit<ParseJsonBodyOptions, 'mode'>`) or remove the options parameter entirely if only fixed strict behavior is intended.

findings.summary: Deprecated shared hook shims remain even though no call sites use them.
evidence: Both files are pure deprecated compatibility re-exports to domain hooks.
Search for imports from `@/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` and `@/shared/hooks/useDerivedItems` returned no matches in `src`, indicating migration appears complete for these paths.
findings.suggestion: Remove both shim files and update any barrel exports/tests that still reference them; if external consumers exist, enforce migration via a short-lived codemod and then delete the aliases.

findings.summary: Deprecated `handleError` API is still heavily used, blocking error-layer migration.
evidence: `handleError.ts` deprecates `handleError`/`handleAndRethrow` in favor of `normalizeAndPresentError` and `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow` with removal target `2026-06-30`.
Repository search shows many production call sites still invoking `handleError(...)`, so the deprecated facade remains the effective API instead of a transitional edge.
findings.suggestion: Run a migration pass replacing `handleError` calls with the new canonical helpers, keep a temporary telemetry counter for remaining legacy invocations, and remove deprecated exports once usage reaches zero.

findings.summary: Several utility modules contain generated-style comment ceremony that obscures logic signal.
evidence: Large multi-section comment blocks restate immediate code behavior (e.g., comments describing straightforward filters, frame midpoint math, and interface fields) rather than documenting hidden invariants.
Repeated header-style comment templates across multiple modules suggest formulaic generation pattern, increasing file length and scanning cost without adding operational guidance.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to only non-obvious constraints, migration rationale, or invariants; remove section-banner and restatement comments in these modules and keep brief intent notes only where behavior is surprising.

findings.summary: Travel-between-images legacy shape types remain in public contracts, extending dual-path maintenance.
evidence: `types.ts` still exports `LegacyStructureVideoConfig` and `TravelBetweenImagesLegacyTaskInput` for migration boundaries.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` and `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` retain full compatibility parsing/enforcement paths and deprecation governance, indicating old+new shapes coexist in active task pipelines.
findings.suggestion: Define a cutoff plan to isolate legacy parsing at a single ingress boundary, stop exporting legacy types from public task type surfaces, and switch downstream callers to canonical `structure_guidance`/`structure_videos` and canonical join-clips mode-specific inputs.

findings.summary: Same hook is implemented in two packages, obscuring ownership and import boundaries.
evidence: Both `src/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts` contain near-identical full logic, not a simple re-export.
Parallel test suites exist for both paths (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/...` and `src/tools/edit-media/hooks/__tests__/...`), reinforcing multiple entrypoints for one concern.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) designate `src/shared/hooks/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts` as canonical. 2) replace `src/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence.ts` with a deprecated re-export shim only. 3) keep one test suite co-located with canonical hook and remove duplicate suite after parity check. 4) update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence|@/tools/edit-media/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence" src | xargs sed -i '' 's#@/shared/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence#@/shared/hooks/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence#g; s#@/tools/edit-media/hooks/useEditToolMediaPersistence#@/shared/hooks/media/useEditToolMediaPersistence#g'`. 5) validate with `pnpm test useEditToolMediaPersistence` and `pnpm typecheck`.

findings.summary: Deprecated domain hooks remain in shared root hooks package, blurring directory boundaries.
evidence: Both assigned hook files are explicit deprecated compatibility shims pointing to `@/domains/generation/hooks/...`.
The shim location keeps domain-owned behavior discoverable under `src/shared/hooks/`, which conflicts with the newer domain package boundary.
`src/shared/hooks/` already has high breadth (`77` files), so retaining migration shims at root increases navigational noise.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) move deprecation shims into `src/shared/hooks/legacy/domainGeneration/` to isolate migration-only files from active hooks. 2) keep shim exports temporarily but add removal deadline comments. 3) migrate all imports to `@/domains/generation/hooks/...` using `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/use(DeleteGenerationWithConfirm|DerivedItems)" src` then update imports. 4) after import count is zero, delete legacy shims. 5) validate with `pnpm test useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm useGalleryPageState` and `pnpm typecheck`.

findings.summary: A settings hook test is placed under generic hooks tests instead of settings hooks tests.
evidence: `useEditVideoSettings.test.ts` lives in `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` but tests a hook from `@/shared/settings/hooks/useEditVideoSettings`.
This placement mixes settings-domain test ownership into a generic hooks test bucket, reducing mirrorability between prod/test paths.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) move `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts` to `src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts`. 2) keep `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` for tests whose production files live in `src/shared/hooks/`. 3) run import fix with `rg -n "useEditVideoSettings" src/shared/settings/hooks/__tests__/useEditVideoSettings.test.ts` and adjust only relative paths if needed. 4) validate with `pnpm test useEditVideoSettings` and `pnpm typecheck`.

findings.summary: High-fan-in contract facades keep legacy and canonical paths coupled in central modules.
evidence: `handleError.ts` exports both canonical and deprecated APIs from one hub (`handleError`, `handleAndRethrow` plus canonical functions), extending shim lifetime on the main import path.
`client.ts` is a top-level facade with only pass-through runtime calls, creating another compatibility choke point rather than narrowing boundaries.
README contract matrix designates these as canonical surfaces, and holistic context shows very high importer counts (240/209/158), amplifying any contract drift.
findings.suggestion: Split compatibility from canonical boundaries in stages: (1) create `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/compat/legacyHandleError.ts` and move deprecated aliases there while keeping canonical exports in `handleError.ts`; (2) add a temporary re-export shim plus deprecation telemetry; (3) migrate imports with `rg "handleAndRethrow|handleError\(" src` and update callers; (4) validate with `npm run test:contracts && npm run test:arch && npm run quality:check`; (5) remove compat entrypoint at the documented removal date.

findings.summary: Governance gate commands are documented, but enforcement for scoped contract files is not explicitly traceable.
evidence: README lists governance test gates but no mapping from each gate to specific contract guarantees (deprecation timeline, bootstrap order, alias behavior).
Scoped contract files depend on comment-level metadata (`@publicContract`, `@deprecated`, `@removalTarget`) without adjacent invariant checks in the module.
These modules are heavily imported, so lack of explicit test-to-contract linkage can let compatibility regressions pass despite gate execution.
findings.suggestion: Add contract-test traceability in two steps: (1) extend README with a table mapping each governance gate to exact contract assertions for `handleError.ts` and `client.ts`; (2) add/label focused tests that assert deprecated alias behavior and Supabase bootstrap/accessor contract, then enforce via `npm run test:contracts` in CI with failure output referencing the mapped contract IDs.

findings.summary: Two core hooks accumulate unrelated concerns and expose oversized controller surfaces.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` owns device detection, pending-frame bookkeeping, drag state, zoom behavior, drop interception, endpoint dragging, video-browser insertion logic, and returns nested controller sections (`viewport`, `drag`, `selection`, `pending`, `drop`, `computed`, `actions`, `endpoint`, `uiState`, `device`).

`useVideoEditing` imports and coordinates settings persistence (`useEditVideoSettings`), LoRA management (`useLoraManager` + `usePublicLoras`), selection lifecycle, validation, and generation/task concerns in one hook, causing UI state and domain workflow logic to change together.
findings.suggestion: Split each hook into focused layers: keep one UI-facing composition hook, but extract pure domain controllers (selection math/validation, timeline interactions, task payload assembly) and side-effect adapters (query/task dispatch, resource loading) into separate modules with narrower contracts.

findings.summary: Submission modes repeat nearly identical orchestration instead of using a shared workflow primitive.
evidence: `submitManaged`, `queueExisting`, and `queueLikeExisting` all repeat the same queue pattern: resolve params, derive label/count, call `queueIncomingTask`, then run `onGenerate` with similar result coercion.
`useFormSubmission` mainly wires refs/builders/command wrappers around the orchestrator, adding another coordination layer without reducing branch complexity in the underlying submit paths.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single `runQueuedSubmission` helper that takes a small strategy object (prompt source, count strategy, pre-submit validation, param builder) and let each mode provide only mode-specific logic; keep `useFormSubmission` as thin composition only after branch de-duplication.

findings.summary: Project lifecycle hook couples domain setup with presentation/runtime concerns.
evidence: `useProjectCRUD` handles user-record provisioning, default project creation, default shot/template copy, CRUD operations, local loading flags, toast/error presentation, and user-settings updates in one place.

`useTasksPaneController` similarly combines persisted UI state (`sessionStorage`), query orchestration, optimistic cache mutation, paging/filter state, and toast/error behavior, indicating repeated controller-hook mixing of data/domain and UI runtime concerns.
findings.suggestion: Separate data workflows from UI concerns: move bootstrap/CRUD/query mutation policies into service-layer hooks (no toast/sessionStorage), then keep pane/project controllers for view state and delegated command invocation only.

findings.summary: Task orchestration files carry multiple policy domains in single modules.
evidence: `orchestrator.ts` mixes segment-gate state-machine logic, sibling discovery strategies, terminal-state transitions, and billing outcome persistence/reconciliation policy in one module.

`individualTravelSegment.ts` and `imageGeneration.ts` each accumulate validation, compatibility parsing, payload composition, resolution/model policy, and task request assembly, producing broad modules where unrelated policy changes co-locate.
findings.suggestion: Refactor task orchestration into explicit layers: `contract parsing/validation`, `workflow state decisions`, and `side-effect executors` (DB updates/billing/task enqueue). Keep each module responsible for one policy domain and compose via small typed interfaces.

findings.summary: Deprecated and new error reporters are both active, fragmenting failure behavior.
evidence: handleError.ts deprecates handleError/handleAndRethrow with removal target 2026-06-30.
useDiscordSignIn.ts and useTasks.ts still call deprecated helpers, while useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts uses coreReporter directly.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate on one reporting API (prefer coreReporter + presentation wrapper), migrate remaining deprecated call sites, and remove deprecated exports once callers are eliminated.

findings.summary: Unknown-total pagination uses Infinity, so next-page prefetch never naturally terminates.
evidence: useAdjacentPagePreloader assigns totalPages = Infinity when totalItems is undefined.
prefetchAdjacentGenerationPages checks currentPage < totalPages for next prefetch; with Infinity this remains true indefinitely.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an explicit hasMore/nextPageKnown state from fetched page payloads and gate prefetch on that signal instead of Infinity.

findings.summary: Non-null assertions and unchecked indexing bypass declared nullable contracts.
evidence: useTasks.ts uses effectiveProjectId! in queryFn despite effectiveProjectId being nullable at the API boundary.
timelineFrameCalculators.ts uses timeline_frame! and draggedShotGens[0].id without a type-level non-empty guarantee.
findings.suggestion: Replace assertions with explicit narrowing helpers and non-empty tuple/types where required; fail fast with typed guard errors when invariants are missing.

findings.summary: UI sibling files diverge on basic string/import style conventions.
evidence: button.tsx and sonner.tsx use double-quote import/style patterns while nearby primitives use single quotes.
Drift occurs within the same `src/shared/components/ui` area, not across separate technology boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Apply one formatter/lint style profile consistently across the UI subtree and auto-format these outlier files to remove local convention drift.
